task.title: Install markdown-validator
task.shortdesc: Install markdown-validator with pip in a Python virtual environment. After installation, the md-validate command is available from your terminal.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Step-by-step instructions to install markdown-validator using pip, verify the installation, and set up a development environment.

ul.li: Python 3.12 or later must be installed. To check your Python version, open a terminal and enter: python --version If Python is not installed or is older than 3.12, download it from python.org.
You need access to a terminal (Command Prompt or PowerShell on Windows, Terminal on macOS or Linux).

step.cmd: Create and activate a virtual environment.

info.p: When the virtual environment is active, your terminal prompt includes (.venv).

step.stepresult: The virtual environment is active. Packages you install now go into this isolated environment.
pip downloads and installs markdown-validator and its dependencies.
The terminal displays the installed version number, confirming that the tool is ready to use.

result.p: markdown-validator is installed. You can now run md-validate validate from this terminal session whenever the virtual environment is active.

postreq.p: If your team's rule set uses sentence-count (s) or part-of-speech (p<N>) rules, also run: Download NLTK language data.
Ready to validate: Validate a single file.
